Helping you shape spaces people love.

Measured building, topographical and utility surveys across the North West. Delivered in as little as one week.

There is a lot of coordination behind getting a project through planning. A big part of that is putting together the right level of information to properly support a scheme, particularly when applications are being scrutinised and every detail is being looked at.

105 Surveys works alongside architects and planning consultants across the North West on measured building surveys, topographical surveys and utility surveys.

We exist to make sure the surveys
supporting your schemes are
accurate, complete, and within budget.

Grade II Listed Case Study

The architects and heritage consultants needed accurate existing drawings before design could begin on a Grade II listed Georgian property.

We carried out a high resolution laser scanning survey covering internal floor plans, external elevations, roof plan, sections, wall thicknesses, door and window positions, ground levels and topographical data. Everything the design team needed to work from with confidence.

Topographical drawing coming soon

Instruction to delivery
Grade II
Listed Georgian property
Approved
Listed building consent and planning

"We're always keen to get 105 Surveys involved early in the project lifecycle. The earlier they're instructed, the better we can assess the design scope and understand what's truly achievable from the building."

— Andrew, SBG LLP

A note from Lewis

Lewis Laycock
Director, 105 Surveys
2026

Alongside 105 Surveys, I carry out my own developments. I have been on the instructing side of surveys, as the client, waiting on a survey to be returned after the agreed timeframe, missing details and site features, all whilst the project sits still and finance costs add up.

That experience shapes how we work. Before we go to site, we confirm exactly what the project needs: the format, the scope, the level of detail. Capturing everything in one visit.

We have worked on a range of schemes across the North West, from residential conversions to listed building works and new build housing. Should any projects be coming up that require a survey to progress design or planning, it would be great to look at it together and make sure your project is properly supported from instruction to delivery.

Lewis Laycock
Director, 105 Surveys
